sourceMovie.title: Carrie
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Carrie works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hereditary
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Hereditary (2018) is the natural next step after Carrie: same horror DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Gerald's Game
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Carrie who respond to its horror will find Gerald's Game (2017) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: The Entity
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Carrie fans keep landing on The Entity (1982) — the horror is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Lords of Chaos
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Lords of Chaos draws from the same horror well as Carrie, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2018 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Burnt Offerings
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Burnt Offerings (1976) captures the horror appeal of Carrie without simply imitating it. The thriller streak running through both films is what puts Burnt Offerings at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Nocturnal Animals
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The thriller appeal of Carrie — that tense edge, the drama — translates cleanly into Nocturnal Animals (2016). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Scream
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Scream (1996) clearly understood what makes horror work — the same quality that keeps Carrie rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Silent Hill
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Silent Hill (2006) is the kind of film Carrie fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The horror overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Road to Perdition
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Road to Perdition shares Carrie's gift for thriller — the 2002 film delivers crime without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: We Need to Talk About Kevin
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Carrie compelling — the thriller, the drama — shows up in We Need to Talk About Kevin (2011) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that tense storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

faq.question: What should I watch after Carrie?
faq.answer: Try Hereditary, Gerald's Game, The Entity—each shares DNA with Carrie while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Carrie?
faq.answer: Carrie (1976) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Carrie part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Carrie Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
